description: Let's move all the controls down below into the tab bar and then we can also move the question indicator down there as well.

results.detailed_description: You asked to move all the Feed's controls down into the tab bar and bring the question indicator down there too. I moved the four toolbar buttons — Settings (gear), the show/hide-question chevron, Browse questions, and Answer — out of the top navigation bar and into a bottom bar that sits right above the tab bar, keeping the same left/right split (gear on the left, the three question controls on the right). I also moved the question header (the prompt text banner) from floating over the top of the feed to floating over the bottom, just above the controls, and flipped its show/hide animation so it slides in and out from the bottom edge. The chevron still points down while the question is showing (tap to collapse) and up when it's hidden (tap to bring it back). One note: iOS doesn't let you put custom buttons inside a TabView's real tab bar, so this uses SwiftUI's bottom toolbar, which renders as its own row directly above the Feed/Answers/Users tabs — the closest thing to 'in the tab bar.' I couldn't run the app to screenshot it (builds happen on the device watcher), so please check the spacing between the bottom bar, the question banner, and the tab bar on your device and let me know if you want anything nudged.
results.summary: Moved the Feed's controls and the question header down to the bottom bar
results.identified_issue: SwiftUI has no API to inject arbitrary buttons into a TabView's actual tab bar, so I used a .bottomBar ToolbarItemGroup, which renders a toolbar row sitting directly above the tab bar — the closest primitive to 'in the tab bar.' If the reporter literally wants the icons interleaved with the Feed/Answers/Users tabs, that isn't achievable with a standard TabView and would need a custom bottom bar; flag if so. The question header was an .overlay(alignment:.top); I flipped it to .bottom and changed its slide transition edge to match. Padding on the header was swapped (top/bottom) so it breathes correctly now that it sits at the bottom.
